channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbreakingNews
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbreakingNews, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Britney Spears arrested in Southern California, Ventura County Sheriff's Office records show
item.description: According to Ventura County inmate records, Spears was arrested around 9:28 p.m. Wednesday and released around 6:07 a.m. on Thursday.

item.title: Iran live updates: US torpedo attack on Iran warship 'an atrocity' minister says
item.description: IDF airstrikes targeted "dozens" of sites connected to internal security forces.

item.title: Noem's testimony on contracts 'false,' Democratic senator claims
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem appeared on Wednesday in front of a House committee.

item.title: Companies are entitled to Trump tariff refunds, trade court rules
item.description: A federal court in New York ruled Wednesday that businesses that paid emergency tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court are eligible for refunds.

item.title: White House insists Iran war won't impact U.S. economy but gas prices are soaring
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt is insisting that the U.S. economy can withstand a prolonged war against Iran, but gas prices and other e...

item.title: US jobless claims filings unchanged from previous week at 213,000, layoffs remain low
item.description: The number of Americans applying for unemployment benefits last week was unchanged from the week before, a sign that layoffs remain at historically lo...

item.title: WATCH: 'Brady Bunch' house designated as historical-cultural monument
item.description: The L.A. City Council designated the iconic home as a historical-cultural monument, protecting the beloved landmark from demolition.

item.title: How Kurdish forces could help the U.S. fight the Iran war
item.description: Kurdish forces surrounding Iran may jump into the fight against the Islamic Republic. CBS News' Courtney Kealy reports.

item.title: Families turn to states for civil rights support as Trump dismantles the Education Department
Massachusetts governor pushes back after RFK Jr. calls out popular coffee chains
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is challenging Starbucks and Dunkin' over the safety of high sugar drinks for teenagers as part of his Make Americ...

item.title: WATCH: Small plane crashes in Phoenix neighborhood
item.description: Officials say the plane took off from a nearby airport before experiencing a mechanical issue and attempting to return before it slammed into two home...

item.title: What to know about the investigation into Catholic priests in Rhode Island and sexual abuse charges
Last 2 names of 6 US soldiers who died in Kuwait attack identified by the Pentagon
item.description: The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa.
